tutorial/request-forms::section-0001::ad.section_text:
To use forms, first install python-multipart .

Make sure you create a virtual environment , activate it, and then install it, for example:

section_text_parts: Form is a class that inherits directly from Body .
tutorial/request-forms::section-0003::ad.section_text: Form is a class that inherits directly from Body .

section_text_parts: To declare form bodies, you need to use Form explicitly, because without it the parameters would be interpreted as query parameters or body (JSON) parameters.
tutorial/request-forms::section-0003::ad.section_text: To declare form bodies, you need to use Form explicitly, because without it the parameters would be interpreted as query parameters or body (JSON) parameters.

tutorial/request-forms::section-0004::ad.title: Technical Details
section_text_parts: Data from forms is normally encoded using the "media type" application/x-www-form-urlencoded .
But when the form includes files, it is encoded as multipart/form-data . You'll read about handling files in the next chapter.
If you want to read more about these encodings and form fields, head to the MDN web docs for POST .
tutorial/request-forms::section-0004::ad.section_text:
Data from forms is normally encoded using the "media type" application/x-www-form-urlencoded .

But when the form includes files, it is encoded as multipart/form-data . You'll read about handling files in the next chapter.

If you want to read more about these encodings and form fields, head to the MDN web docs for POST .

section_text_parts: You can declare multiple Form parameters in a path operation , but you can't also declare Body fields that you expect to receive as JSON, as the request will have the body encoded using application/x-www-form-urlencoded instead of application/json .
This is not a limitation of FastAPI , it's part of the HTTP protocol.
tutorial/request-forms::section-0004::ad.section_text:
You can declare multiple Form parameters in a path operation , but you can't also declare Body fields that you expect to receive as JSON, as the request will have the body encoded using application/x-www-form-urlencoded instead of application/json .

This is not a limitation of FastAPI , it's part of the HTTP protocol.
